persona_json.name: Derek, the Infrastructure-First Platform Engineer
persona_json.summary: Derek is a Senior DevOps Engineer or Platform Engineer at a mid-sized SaaS/fintech company who treats infrastructure as code as non-negotiable. He evaluates software based on how it integrates into his existing Terraform and CI/CD pipelines, not the other way around—manual UI workflows are deal-breakers, and he expects APIs, webhooks, and SDKs to be built for programmatic consumption, not UI supplementation.

demographics.education_level: Bachelor's in Computer Science or equivalent

goals: Fully integrate sales/marketing automation into Terraform state management to eliminate manual infrastructure configuration
Automate data export and sync workflows via APIs or webhooks instead of relying on CSV downloads
Build custom dashboards and reporting pipelines that pull data programmatically without UI interactions
Implement GitHub-native integrations so platform automation flows through existing CI/CD gates
Reduce operational overhead by shifting from UI-driven setup to declarative, infrastructure-as-code approaches
pains: Manual UI-based configuration is incompatible with infrastructure-as-code philosophy and slows deployment cycles
CSV bulk exports are static and non-scalable for real-time data synchronization needs
SDK documentation assumes web app usage, leaving gaps for infrastructure engineers integrating via API/code
GitHub integrations that still require manual UI clicks defeat the purpose of automation
Tools that force a choice between UI workflows or API-only approaches create inconsistent onboarding experiences
Lack of native Terraform provider means managing sales/marketing state outside version control
motivations: Everything-as-code mindset: configuration must be declarative, reproducible, and version-controlled
Time-to-production velocity: any manual step in the deployment pipeline is a bottleneck
Operational resilience: systems that scale programmatically are more reliable than UI-driven workflows
Team standardization: wants all integrations to follow the same infrastructure-as-code patterns across the stack
API-first thinking: values tools with robust, well-documented APIs over feature-rich UIs
objections: Why would I set this up manually through the UI when it should integrate with my Terraform state?
If this requires clicking around in a dashboard to configure, we're solving the wrong problem.
Bulk exports via CSV downloads are not a real solution for automation; we need streaming or programmatic access.
We tried the SDK, but the onboarding docs assume you're using the web app—not helpful for infrastructure code.
If GitHub integration means I still have to click things in the product, that's just adding complexity.
not_this: Wouldn't adopt a tool just because it has a polished UI or clickable workflows—needs to fit the infrastructure stack first
Doesn't trust CSV-based integrations or one-off API calls as 'real automation'
Wouldn't accept documentation written exclusively for business users or non-technical stakeholders
Won't champion a product that requires end-users to learn a new platform instead of using existing tools
Doesn't view webhooks or SDKs as 'nice-to-have' extras—they're baseline requirements
channels: Technical blogs and Medium (infrastructure patterns, observability)
Terraform registry and IaC community forums
Product documentation sites and SDK repositories
decision_triggers: Native Terraform provider or HCL support lands in the roadmap
Proof that webhooks and APIs can fully replace manual UI configuration
Documentation explicitly written for infrastructure engineers, not just app users
GitHub Actions or CI-native integration that requires zero manual steps
Ability to export/sync data programmatically in real-time (not batch CSV)
sample_quotes: We manage everything as code—if you can't Terraform it, it's not part of our stack.
The API should be the primary interface; the UI is just a convenience for business users.
If I have to click buttons in your platform to automate something, you've missed the point of automation.
We need streaming or programmatic access to data, not CSV dumps that go stale in minutes.
Your onboarding works great for product managers. We need docs written for people reading your Swagger spec.

journey_stages.mindset: Skeptical of marketing/sales tools by default; evaluates based on whether APIs and Terraform integration exist before engaging
key_actions: Checks for Terraform provider availability
Reviews API documentation before considering contact
Searches for GitHub integration capabilities
Looks for mentions of webhook support in feature lists

journey_stages.mindset: Hands-on evaluation mode; spends significant time exploring webhooks, CI pipelines, and SDK behavior in isolated environments
key_actions: Sets up webhook configurations to test event delivery
Attempts SDK installation and integration
Explores CI pipeline integration documentation
Tests GitHub integration workflows
Builds custom dashboards with API data pulls
content_preferences: SDK release notes and API changelogs
Technical deep dives on webhook reliability

journey_stages.mindset: Ready to buy only if infrastructure integration path is clear; may defer if docs or provider support feels incomplete
key_actions: Demands proof that UI-free configuration is possible
Requires documentation written for code-first teams
Negotiates for Terraform provider development or timeline
Sets up API access and validates programmatic workflows
Tests bulk export alternatives (streaming, webhooks, polling)
content_preferences: Customer success stories from infrastructure-first teams

source_evidence.claim: Wants to fully integrate sales/marketing automation into Terraform state management
Automate data export and sync workflows via APIs or webhooks instead of CSVs
Build custom dashboards and reporting pipelines that pull data programmatically
Implement GitHub-native integrations through CI/CD gates
Reduce operational overhead by shifting from UI-driven to infrastructure-as-code approaches
Manual UI-based configuration is incompatible with infrastructure-as-code philosophy
CSV bulk exports are static and non-scalable for real-time data synchronization
SDK documentation assumes web app usage, leaving gaps for infrastructure engineers
GitHub integrations that still require manual UI clicks defeat automation purpose
Lack of native Terraform provider means managing sales/marketing state outside version control

persona_json.name: Growth Marketer Gavi
persona_json.summary: Gavi is a hands-on Growth Marketing Manager at a mid-market SaaS company (50-200 employees) who spends significant time in email composition, audience segmentation, and A/B testing. Frustrated by broken attribution tracking and manual reporting friction, she's searching for a platform that lets her move fast, validate ideas through testing, and prove ROI with clean campaign-level data.
goals: Set up and launch campaigns in rapid cycles to test messaging and audience combinations
Build sophisticated audience segments without requiring engineering or data team help
Reduce time spent on manual report generation and data exports
Run rapid A/B tests to optimize campaign performance
Prove marketing ROI with clear, attribution-based reporting
Improve campaign personalization through intelligent audience segmentation
pains: Attribution tracking across campaigns is inconsistent and unreliable
Manual report generation consumes hours that could be spent on strategy
Campaign performance insights are delayed because of reporting friction
Limited visibility into which campaigns actually drive conversions
Spending too much time in admin/data tasks instead of strategy
UTM and campaign-level metrics are missing or buried in reports
motivations: Wants to prove ROI of marketing efforts with clean, attributable data
Driven by the need to move fast and validate ideas quickly
Motivated to optimize campaign performance through test-and-learn velocity
Values efficiency: wants to eliminate repetitive admin work
Eager to segment audiences intelligently to improve personalization
Wants to justify marketing spend to leadership with hard numbers
objections: Platforms that promise features but have poor or delayed attribution
Overly complex UIs that require training or documentation to use
Platforms that require learning another interface for basic audience builds
Lack of granular UTM and campaign-level reporting out of the box
High setup friction that delays time-to-first-test
not_this: Wouldn't settle for 'good enough' attribution—needs to trace every conversion to a specific campaign
Doesn't want to hire a data analyst just to answer basic campaign questions
Won't adopt a platform that requires engineers to set up audiences or UTM tracking
Rolls her eyes at vanity metrics—only cares about conversion-based ROI
Refuses to spend weeks in setup; needs to run her first test within days
decision_triggers: Platform demonstrates clear, campaign-level attribution out of the box
Easy audience segmentation without engineering handoff
Can launch first A/B test within 2-3 days of signing up
UTM and campaign metrics are native to reporting, not an afterthought
Reporting is fast enough to inform daily optimization decisions
Leadership buys into the ROI story because of clean attribution
sample_quotes: Attribution tracking across campaigns is a mess.
Report exports should include UTM breakdown by default.
I need to prove ROI to leadership—they won't accept 'we think it worked.'
I can't spend three weeks in setup; I need to run tests this week.
If I have to build audiences in a separate tool, we're not using this platform.

journey_stages.mindset: Frustrated with current platform's attribution and reporting gaps; actively looking for alternatives that solve this
key_actions: Visiting /landing_page to understand product positioning
Reading product docs and case studies focused on attribution
Checking support forums for UTM and campaign reporting features
content_preferences: Case studies showing how other growth teams prove ROI

journey_stages.mindset: Evaluating whether this platform will reduce reporting friction and offer superior attribution; skeptical about setup time
key_actions: Spending extended time in /audiences/builder to test segment workflow
Setting up /experiments/new to gauge ease of A/B testing
Requesting demo or trial to validate attribution fidelity

journey_stages.mindset: Ready to buy if she can validate that attribution is real, setup is fast, and ROI reporting is native
key_actions: Building a real audience segment in the tool
Running a small A/B test campaign
Exporting a report and checking for UTM + campaign-level metrics
Negotiating onboarding timeline to ensure fast activation
content_preferences: Customer success stories with attribution metrics
